title: Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome
chief_complaint: Term infant with irritability, high-pitched cry, poor feeding, sneezing, and diarrhea. Mother has history of opioid use disorder on methadone maintenance.

physical_exam.General: Irritable, inconsolable. High-pitched cry. Tremors. Hyperactive Moro reflex.
physical_exam.Skin: Excoriations on face from rubbing. Diaper dermatitis.
physical_exam.GI: Frequent watery stools. Vomiting. Poor feeding (takes 20mL then pulls away).
physical_exam.Neurologic: Tremors, hypertonia, myoclonic jerks. Seizure activity absent.

hidden_data.finnegan: Finnegan Neonatal Abstinence Score 16 (pharmacologic treatment indicated ≥8 ×2 consecutive scores)
hidden_data.toxicology: Infant urine: Methadone positive. Maternal: Methadone prescribed. No other substances.

correct_diagnosis: Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome (NAS) from In-Utero Methadone Exposure
key_learning_points: NAS = withdrawal syndrome in newborns after in-utero exposure to opioids (heroin, methadone, buprenorphine, prescribed opioids). Incidence quadrupled in last decade
Symptoms: CNS (irritability, high-pitched cry, tremors, seizures), GI (poor feeding, vomiting, diarrhea, weight loss), Autonomic (sweating, sneezing, yawning, fever, tachypnea)
Finnegan score: Systematic scoring q3-4h. Score ≥8 ×2 consecutive = pharmacologic treatment. Score <8 for 24-48h = wean and discharge

Treatment: Non-pharmacologic FIRST (swaddling, low stimulation, rooming-in, breastfeeding if mother stable on MAT). Pharmacologic: Morphine or Methadone (first-line). Phenobarbital as adjunct for polysubstance exposure. Clonidine emerging as alternative

Breastfeeding encouraged if mother on stable methadone/buprenorphine (minimal transfer in breastmilk). Contraindicated if HIV, active substance use, or multiple substances. Mother-infant dyad should stay together (rooming-in reduces need for pharmacotherapy by 50%)
